title: Welcome | Polyglot Guide
excerpt: Welcome to the Polyglot Guide | Meta Mode Guide Welcome to Meta Mode — the centralized documentation for the framework and build-system features. This mode is dedicated to explaining the inner workings of the Polyglot Dev Atlas. It serves a

title: All Showcases
excerpt: Explore curated presentation styles demonstrating layouts and patterns for tutorials, references, galleries, playgrounds, and IDE-like pages. Use the list to the left to open a specific showcase.

title: Hierarchical Structure
excerpt: Nesting & Recursive Blocks The Atlas engine is built on a recursive rendering logic. A blocks element can contain an array of other blocks, allowing you to create deeply nested documentation hierarchies. To maintai

title: Content Element Library
excerpt: Element Showcase The engine processes a specific set of JSON objects. Each type maps to a semantic HTML structure designed for technical documentation. Standard paragraphs use type: 'text'. Headings use type: 'title'

title: Styling & Customization
excerpt: The Three-Tier Style System The Polyglot Atlas v2 separates structure from aesthetics. You can control the 'feel' of the interface through three different layers of customization. The Style Picker in the navbar controls the physical propert

title: Meta: Getting Started
excerpt: Getting Started with Meta Mode This short page demonstrates the features available in Meta Mode.

title: Gallery Style Showcase
excerpt: A visual-forward layout for screenshots, art, and UI showcases. Optimize images for web for faster loading.

title: IDE Style Showcase
excerpt: A compact, developer-focused skin that emphasizes monospaced type, clear code blocks, and strong contrast for editors. Use a narrow column width for reading diffs.

title: Playground Style Showcase
excerpt: An exploratory style for interactive demos and small experiments. Experiment outputs may vary across browsers.

title: Reference Style Showcase
excerpt: Dense, information-first layout suitable for API references and dense docs. Use in-page search for quick lookup.
